entries.title: Morals on the Book of Job, Book XXIII on job 33:8
entries.excerpt:
Thou hast spoken then in mine ears, and I have heard the voice of thy words.

And subjoining the very words, he says what he heard.

entries.title: Commentary on Job on job 33:8
entries.excerpt: After he has said these things as an introduction, he strengthens the arguments which he intends to use against Job, and so he says, "Therefore you have spoken in my hearing," as if to say: You cannot excuse yourself by saying that you did not say this, "and I heard the sound of your words," for I listened attentively.
